---
title: "3.5 Methods: How to Write Them"
subtitle: "Part 4: Class Pet Challenge"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit3-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Challenge Context

### Build a class with useful behavior

![Animal clinic design prompt](../../assets/3-class-creation/3-5-methods-how-to-write-them/animalclinic.png){fig-align="center" width="42%"}

The Class Pet challenge asks you to combine:

::: {.tight-list}
- private instance variables
- a constructor
- getters
- setters or mutators
- `toString`
- methods that compute or update state
:::

## Starter Class

### Keep state private

```java
public class Pet
{
    private String name;
    private int age;
    private int hunger;

    public Pet(String petName, int petAge)
    {
        name = petName;
        age = petAge;
        hunger = 5;
    }
}
```

Start with state that the methods can read or change.

## Add Getters

### Expose selected values

```java
public String getName()
{
    return name;
}

public int getAge()
{
    return age;
}

public int getHunger()
{
    return hunger;
}
```

These are non-`void` methods with return values.

## Add Mutators

### Change state in controlled ways

```java
public void feed()
{
    if (hunger > 0)
    {
        hunger--;
    }
}

public void play()
{
    hunger++;
}
```

The method names describe actions in the domain of the class.

## Add `toString`

### Make object state readable

```java
public String toString()
{
    return name + " is " + age + " years old and has hunger " + hunger;
}
```

Use `toString` to check whether method calls changed the object as expected.

## Full Code Page

### State, constructor, and getters

```java
public class Pet
{
    private String name;
    private int age;
    private int hunger;

    public Pet(String petName, int petAge)
    {
        name = petName;
        age = petAge;
        hunger = 5;
    }

    public String getName()
    {
        return name;
    }

    public int getAge()
    {
        return age;
    }
}
```

## Full Code Page

### Mutators and `toString`

```java
public class Pet
{
    private String name;
    private int age;
    private int hunger;

    public void feed()
    {
        if (hunger > 0)
        {
            hunger--;
        }
    }

    public void play()
    {
        hunger++;
    }

    public String toString()
    {
        return name + " is " + age + " years old and has hunger " + hunger;
    }
}
```

## Focused Trace

### Track state changes

```java
Pet p = new Pet("Coco", 3);
System.out.println(p);
p.feed();
p.play();
p.play();
System.out.println(p);
```

Trace:

| Step | `hunger` |
|---|---:|
| after constructor | 5 |
| after `feed()` | 4 |
| after first `play()` | 5 |
| after second `play()` | 6 |

## Community Class Extension

### Continue your own class

For the class you designed earlier, add:

::: {.tight-list}
- one getter
- one mutator
- one method with a parameter
- one `toString` method
:::

Each method should support the purpose of the class, not just exist to satisfy a task list.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- combine constructor, getters, mutators, and `toString` in one class
- explain how each method supports the class design
- trace how method calls change instance variable values
- keep instance variables private and access them through methods
- avoid adding public setters that do not make sense for the object's model
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
